text.title: REST API Design Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the REST API Design methodology. Every rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale, plus the universal skip-this-methodology gate.

rule.statement: URLs MUST use plural nouns (`/users`, `/orders`); verbs in path (`/getUser`, `/doThing`) are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Verb-in-URL leaks server method names; nouns let HTTP methods carry the action and survive refactors.

rule.statement: GET = read (safe + idempotent), POST = create (non-idempotent), PUT = full replace (idempotent), PATCH = partial update, DELETE = remove (idempotent); violations forbidden.
rule.rationale: Semantics let caches, retries, and proxies behave correctly; mixing breaks all three.

rule.statement: Response MUST use the correct status: 200 (read/update), 201 (create with body), 204 (no body), 400 (client validation), 401 (auth), 403 (permission), 404 (resource missing), 409 (conflict), 422 (semantic validation), 429 (rate), 500 (server). 200 with error body is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Status codes drive client SDK retry logic + monitoring dashboards; 200-on-error breaks both.

rule.statement: Collections MUST use cursor (keyset) pagination above 1k rows; offset pagination beyond 1k is rejected.

rationale.source: Stripe API docs; Postgres OFFSET-perf studies

rule.rationale: OFFSET scans + discards on every page request; cursor uses an index seek regardless of depth.

rule.statement: Error responses MUST follow RFC 7807 (`application/problem+json`) with `type`, `title`, `status`, `detail`, `instance`; plain strings or ad-hoc shapes are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Problem+JSON lets clients route on `type` URI and surface human-friendly `title` consistently.

rule.statement: API path MUST carry an explicit version segment (`/v1/users`); header- or query-based versioning is forbidden unless documented exception.
rule.rationale: Path versioning is grep-able and cache-friendly; header-only versioning is invisible to load balancers and proxies.

rule.statement: Collection filters MUST be expressed via query params (`?status=active`); filters in path or POST bodies for GETs are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Query params keep collection URIs cacheable and discoverable; embedded filters break caches and proxies.

rule.statement: Apply this methodology only if all entries in `## Applies If` hold and none of `## Skip If` triggers; otherwise abort and route to the correct methodology.

rationale.source: F-066 spec - decision tree minimum form

rule.rationale: A misrouted methodology produces wrong-shape output; the explicit skip rule is the universal default branch from the decision tree.
